text.title: Creating or Updating Claude Code Commands Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the commands methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Command body MUST be ≤ 250 lines (ideal ≤ 150). Past 250 lines, split into a Skill.
rule.rationale: Long commands consume prompt budget and become unreadable; the cap forces decomposition.

rule.statement: Commands with arguments MUST declare them in a fenced `<arguments>` block with name + type + required flag. Argument parsing relies on the declared shape.
rule.rationale: Implicit argument parsing leads to bugs when users pass values in unexpected order.

rule.statement: Live context (git branch, diff, env vars) MUST be injected via `!` prefix bash execution within the command body, NOT hardcoded.
rule.rationale: Hardcoded context goes stale; !-prefix evaluates at /invoke time.

rule.statement: Command MUST whitelist the minimum tools needed. Defaulting to all tools is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Tight whitelist is the primary safety mechanism for /invoke actions.

rule.statement: Command body MUST be terse: bullet lists over tables, English over non-English, NO ASCII borders, no marketing adjectives.
rule.rationale: Every byte of command body lives in context window on /invoke; padding wastes the budget.
